trade.summary: Significant bilateral trade between two G7 members. Canada exports natural resources, aircraft, and agricultural products to Germany. Germany exports vehicles (BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen), machinery, chemicals, and pharmaceuticals to Canada. CETA has deepened trade ties.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent bilateral relations. Both are G7 members, NATO allies, and share strong democratic values. Canada-Germany cooperation is intensive on Ukraine support, climate policy, and multilateral institutions. The German-Canadian energy partnership (hydrogen and LNG) has been a major recent focus.

military.summary: Both are NATO allies with close defense cooperation. Canada leads NATO's Enhanced Forward Presence battlegroup in Latvia (adjacent to German commitments). Joint exercises and intelligence sharing. Germany has invested in Canadian defense procurement.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. German automotive (BMW, Volkswagen, Mercedes) and chemical companies (BASF, Bayer) have major Canadian operations. Canadian banks and pension funds invest in Germany. CETA has boosted investment flows.

energy.summary: Canada-Germany signed a hydrogen and energy cooperation agreement in 2022 as Germany sought to diversify away from Russian gas. Canada has pledged to export LNG and hydrogen to Germany. First LNG shipment milestone under active development.

scientific.summary: Very strong scientific cooperation. German and Canadian universities have numerous partnerships. DFG-NSERC joint programs span physics, chemistry, materials science, and environmental research. Large German-Canadian academic community.
